message:
feat(raptorq): optimize gf256 mul/addmul hot loops (bd-1kdw)

Three optimizations to the GF(256) encode/decode hot path:

1. Lower MUL_TABLE_THRESHOLD from 256 to 64 — the 255-lookup table
   build amortises earlier than previously set.

2. Add mul_with_table_wide() — batch 8 table lookups per iteration
   in gf256_mul_slice, matching the existing addmul wide pattern.

3. Rewrite addmul_with_table_wide() using chunks_exact_mut/chunks_exact
   iterators for bounds-check elision in the hot loop.

Benchmark results (16 KiB):
  mul_slice:    1.66–1.70 GiB/s (was ~1.1 GiB/s, +50%)
  addmul_slice: 2.08–2.19 GiB/s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
